title: carduus segetum
intro: Carduus segetum, also known as the wild artichoke or cornflower thistle, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family that grows in fields and meadows. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which help to reduce swelling and improve kidney function. The medicinal properties of Carduus segetum have been associated with various health benefits, including the reduction of blood pressure, improvement of digestive health, and relief from menstrual cramps and other symptoms related to PMS. Carduus segetum is typically prepared as an infusion, tincture, or decoction, and has been traditionally used to support overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
